mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.0 percent within a single month, labor market deterioration triggers immediate risk-off positioning by institutional investors, depressing S&P 500 prices over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0 percent, or the signal exhibits no predictive power over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a decline in real GDP growth signals a synchronized supply-demand mismatch that compresses corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns remain positive when both INDPRO and GDPC1 decline concurrently, or the interaction term shows zero correlation with equity returns across three full market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid policy tightening, elevated short-term rates increase borrowing costs for highly leveraged corporations, which directly reduces earnings per share and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises more than 0.25 percentage points in a single meeting
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral during consecutive 25-basis-point rate hikes, or the hypothesis fails to outperform a random baseline over a 10-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential increase in new housing starts reflects strengthening consumer confidence and credit availability, which boosts forward-looking equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates negative 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of months where HOUST increases sequentially, or the rolling correlation between housing starts and equity returns falls below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index falls below 105.0, manufacturing capacity constraints and weak order books force corporate guidance cuts, which systematically depresses S&P 500 valuations over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly equity returns remain positive when INDPRO sustains levels below 105.0, or the threshold signal yields a negative information coefficient across multiple economic cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a rise in RSAFS confirms robust aggregate demand, which sustains earnings momentum and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when both PAYEMS and RSAFS expand simultaneously, or the joint signal fails to generate a positive rank IC over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a disinflationary environment where CPI growth falls below 2.0 percent annually, the Federal Reserve maintains accommodative policy, which lowers equity risk premiums and lifts S&P 500 prices over the subsequent week.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL annualized growth rate falls below 2.0 percent
failure_condition: Equity returns decline when CPI growth remains below 2.0 percent, or the hypothesis produces a negative Sharpe ratio when applied to out-of-sample data spanning two decades.
